name: Sourdough Bread
recipeInstructions:
DOUGH
1 1/2 cups primary batter
1/4 cup milk
1 tablespoon butter
1 tablespoon sugar
3 cups all-purpose flour (unbleached)
1/2 teaspoon salt
PRIMARY BATTER
Combine the starter, water and flour in a large bowl. Mix well with a whisk and cover with Saran Wrap. Allow to stand for 12-24 hours until completely fermented. The longer the proofing period, the more sour the final product will be. After this proofing period, remove 1 cup of the batter and return it to your jar of sourdough starter. Put the starter jar back in the fridge.
Dough
Place the remainder of the primary batter (about 1 1/2 cups) and all other ingredients in the mixer in the order presented. Mix on a low speed until just combined, then switch to a high speed and mix for 3-5 minutes. Turn off the machine and allow the dough to rise until doubled in bulk, about 2-2 1/2 hours.
FOR A LOAF
When the second rising is complete, remove the dough from the mixer, form into a loaf, place into a greased 9x5 inch bread pan, and allow to double in bulk. Bake at 375°F for about 25-30 minutes.
FOR BAGUETTES
When the second rising is complete, remove the dough from the mixer and divide it into two equal pieces. Form each piece into two 15-inch loaves and place them in baguette pans or on a greased cookie sheet. Allow the loaves to double in bulk. Slash diagonally several times and bake at 400°F for about 18-20 minutes. For a crisper crust, just before placing the baguettes in the oven, spray them with cold water. Then spray them every three minutes for nine minutes.
